seo.page_title: Nissan Leaf 2024 import to NZ | Japan auction
seo.meta_description: Import a 2024 Nissan Leaf to NZ with clear costs, known battery health and WLTP range around 270–385 km, depending on 39–62 kWh battery options.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2024 Nissan Leaf to NZ
seo.og_title: Import a 2024 Nissan Leaf from Japan to NZ
seo.og_description: Bring a 2024 Nissan Leaf into NZ with transparent costs, focus on battery health and WLTP range around 270–385 km from 39–62 kWh battery options.

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2024 Nissan Leaf to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring a 2024 Nissan Leaf into NZ with clear pricing, known battery health and WLTP range that works for local roads.
proof_points: Direct access to major Japanese EV auctions
Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2024 Nissan Leaf suits daily NZ driving, from school runs to motorway commutes. With 39–62 kWh battery options and WLTP ranges around 270–385 km depending on model, most owners can cover a full day’s use on a single charge. Lower EV running costs and quiet driving make it easy to live with around town and on the open road.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes on a 2024 Nissan Leaf import
savings_block.body: For a 2024 Nissan Leaf, your total landed cost starts with the Japanese auction price, then adds shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee. We lay out each line item before you commit, so you can see how the auction hammer price turns into a realistic NZ‑landed figure. Because 2024 Leafs are still new in Japan, we’ll talk through how grade, mileage and battery size affect the final range of landed prices.

cta_form.headline: Find your 2024 Nissan Leaf — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: See how the 2024 Leaf’s digital dash shows remaining range and charging status, so you can plan your trips without guesswork.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2024 Nissan Leaf offers WLTP ranges around 270–385 km, depending on whether you choose the 39 kWh or 62 kWh battery, paired with motors in the 110–160 kW range. That’s enough for most NZ commutes and weekend trips without needing to charge during the day. With no non-EV costs and fewer moving parts than a traditional car, day‑to‑day running costs are typically lower. Buying from Japan gives access to a wide spread of grades and mileage, so we can focus on strong battery health and the features you actually want.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’re likely doing a regular commute, school runs and weekend trips, and want to cut your running costs without overcomplicating life. You’re happy to charge at home overnight and use public fast chargers on longer drives. If you want a modern hatchback EV with 39–62 kWh battery options and WLTP range around 270–385 km, the 2024 Leaf is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been helping Kiwis bring cars in from Japan since 2009, including a growing number of EVs like the Nissan Leaf. You get full auction documentation, photos and our commentary before you decide to bid. We explain the costs from Japan auction through to NZ‑landed, including certification and compliance steps. A real NZ team stays in touch from first enquiry through to the car arriving here.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Suited to shorter commutes and city use, with WLTP range around 270 km while keeping the upfront cost lower.
Gives WLTP range around 385 km, better for longer commutes, regional trips and more flexibility between charges.
Enough power for safe overtakes on NZ highways and confident merging, while still being easy to drive in town.
No non-EV costs, fewer moving parts and smoother driving in stop‑start traffic.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Japanese domestic market safety and tech features
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Many 2024 Leafs include driver‑assist systems and modern infotainment that work well for NZ conditions, subject to each car’s spec.

cta_mid_page.headline: Tell us how you’ll use your Leaf
cta_mid_page.subtext: Share your daily driving and budget and we’ll suggest 2024 Leaf options from Japan that make sense.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how key 2024 Nissan Leaf variants differ when you’re choosing what to import.

rows.notes: This page focuses on 2024 models, which meet current ESC and safety expectations. Being new, they typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted.
The main choices are around 39 kWh and 62 kWh batteries. The 39 kWh suits shorter daily use, while the 62 kWh Leaf e+ offers more range and stronger performance for longer trips.
Higher trims in Japan can add features like more advanced driver‑assist systems, upgraded interiors and extra convenience tech. We match trims to your priorities rather than chasing badges alone.

As a late‑model EV, a 2024 Leaf typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean. We factor in entry certification, any work required at compliance and current fees when estimating landed cost.
2024 Leafs are available but not as common as older years, and 62 kWh e+ models can be tighter in supply. That can mean waiting a bit longer or adjusting budget to secure the right car.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief, talk through battery size, range needs and budget, and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and other major auctions in Japan. For a Leaf, we pay close attention to battery health notes, photos of the dash and any EV‑specific comments on each shortlisted car.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet, photos and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common trims or 62 kWh e+ models, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance as required. Once that’s complete, we arrange delivery within NZ or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2024 Leaf is straightforward, but it helps to understand the main moving parts.

items.risk: Worry about battery health and future range.
items.solution: We focus on 2024 Leafs with strong auction documentation, clear photos and, where available, SOH information. We explain what that means for real‑world range in NZ before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Concern about hidden costs between auction price and NZ‑landed.
items.solution: We break down auction price, shipping, insurance, port charges, entry certification, compliance work, our fee and on‑road costs separately, so you can see where every dollar goes.

items.risk: Uncertainty about auction grades and what they really mean.
items.solution: We interpret the auction sheet for you, including EV‑specific notes, and highlight any comments that might affect condition, battery or compliance, so you can make an informed call.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us how far you drive, what you want from a 2024 Leaf and what you’re comfortable spending. We shortlist suitable cars from Japan, explain the documentation and costs, then only bid when you’re ready. If you’d like to explore options, send through a brief and we’ll come back with real examples.

faq_block.q: What WLTP range can I expect from a 2024 Nissan Leaf in NZ?
faq_block.a: A 2024 Leaf with the 39 kWh battery is around 270 km WLTP, while the 62 kWh Leaf e+ is around 385 km WLTP. Real‑world range in NZ will vary with speed, terrain, weather and how you drive.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2024 Nissan Leaf from Japan?
faq_block.a: Total cost depends on battery size, trim, mileage and auction price on the day. We start with the Japan auction price, then add shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee to give you a realistic landed range before you commit.

faq_block.q: Will a 2024 Nissan Leaf meet NZ entry standards?
faq_block.a: A 2024 Leaf is a late‑model EV and usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Final approval is only confirmed after physical inspection and entry certification in NZ.

faq_block.q: How long does it take for a 2024 Leaf to arrive in NZ?
faq_block.a: Once we win an auction, shipping is typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. Allow extra time for booking space on a vessel, port processing and NZ entry certification before you can drive it.

faq_block.q: What auction grade should I look for on a 2024 Leaf?
faq_block.a: Higher grades usually mean tidier condition, but the auction sheet comments and photos matter just as much. We explain the grade, point out any notes about damage or EV‑related issues and share the documentation so you can decide what you’re comfortable with.

footer_cta.headline: Start your 2024 Nissan Leaf search
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us your budget, battery preference and how far you drive, and we’ll come back with real 2024 Leaf options from Japan and full landed pricing.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on Nissan Leaf auctions, EV import tips and example landed costs direct to your inbox.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ‑based import specialist working directly with Japanese auctions since 2009. We handle a mix of EVs and other vehicles, focusing on clear documentation and cost breakdowns. Our role is to help you choose and land a car that suits how you actually drive.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost for a 2024 Nissan Leaf covers more than just the auction hammer price.

rows.notes: The winning bid in Japan, influenced by battery size, trim, mileage, grade and demand for 2024 Leafs.
Ocean freight from Japan to NZ plus marine insurance to cover the car while it’s in transit.
NZ port charges, inspection, entry certification and any work needed to meet local standards, along with our service fee.

auction_checklist.check: Battery size and variant (39 kWh vs 62 kWh Leaf e+)
auction_checklist.why: Determines WLTP range, performance and typical auction price, so it’s central to whether the car suits your driving.

auction_checklist.check: Battery health indicators and EV‑specific notes
auction_checklist.why: Comments, SOH readings and dash photos help gauge remaining usable range and long‑term ownership prospects.
Shows how tidy the car is and whether there’s damage that could affect value or require repair in NZ.
Lower mileage and documented servicing usually support better long‑term reliability and battery condition.
Ensures you’re getting the level of tech you expect for NZ roads, such as driver‑assist systems and parking aids.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You regularly drive very long distances in areas with limited public charging and don’t want to plan around range or charging stops.
You’re looking for the absolute lowest upfront cost and are comfortable with an older EV that may have more battery degradation.
You prefer a larger SUV‑style EV with more ground clearance and cargo space than a hatchback like the Leaf offers.

alternatives_block.intro: If a 2024 Leaf doesn’t quite line up with your range, size or budget needs, it can be worth considering other EVs.

rows.better_if: You want a Leaf but need to keep the upfront cost down and are open to a slightly older car.
rows.tradeoff: Lower purchase price but potentially more battery wear and older tech compared with a 2024 model.

battery_health_block.headline: Battery health on a 2024 Nissan Leaf
battery_health_block.body: On a 2024 Leaf, the 39 kWh and 62 kWh batteries should still be early in their life, but it’s worth checking every car carefully. We use auction sheets, photos and any available SOH data to avoid surprises and talk through what that means for your expected WLTP‑style range in NZ conditions.
checklist: Look for SOH (state of health) readings or dash photos where available, especially on higher‑mileage cars.
Check auction comments for notes about rapid charging habits, battery warnings or EV system alerts.
Favour cars with consistent service history and no mention of EV system repairs unless clearly resolved.
Consider your daily distance and choose 39 kWh or 62 kWh with a buffer, not right on the limit.
Ask us to explain how the documented battery health is likely to feel in your typical NZ driving.

charging_block.headline: Charging a 2024 Nissan Leaf in NZ
charging_block.home_charging: Most 2024 Leaf owners in NZ charge at home overnight using a dedicated wall unit, topping up the 39 kWh or 62 kWh battery while power demand is lower. We can talk through typical overnight charge times and what sort of home setup makes sense for your place.
charging_block.public_charging: For longer trips, you can use public fast chargers to add useful range in a short stop, especially with the 62 kWh Leaf e+. We’ll help you think about how often you’re likely to use fast charging based on your routes and where chargers are on your usual paths.
caveats: Charging speeds and connector types can vary by market, so we confirm what the specific car supports from the auction details.
Frequent fast charging can affect long‑term battery health, so it’s best treated as a top‑up tool rather than your only charging method.
If you live in an apartment or can’t install a home charger, we’ll discuss whether public charging access near you is practical for day‑to‑day use.
